reason: rope flatten last resort: borrowed-slice readers (resolveData) cannot propagate errors; flattenRopeNode retries after one object-cycle collection and only a second allocation failure aborts (eecf6c8)
exit_milestone: make the remaining borrowed-slice readers rope-aware or fallible (String.ensureFlat first) so the infallible flatten backstop can be deleted

contains: @panic("class table mutation from non-owner Runtime thread")
reason: class-table mutation is restricted to the Runtime owner thread; this internal invariant catches API misuse after checked public mutation boundaries and is unrelated to allocation failure
exit_milestone: retain until the class-table mutation API contract changes so every internal caller can propagate WrongRuntimeThread instead of relying on an owner-thread invariant

reason: unchecked internal Runtime mutation requires the owner thread; this invariant catches API misuse behind the checked requireOwnerThread boundary and is unrelated to allocation failure
exit_milestone: retain until the Runtime mutation API contract changes so every internal mutation path can propagate WrongRuntimeThread without an asserting owner-only tier

reason: destroying a Runtime with live local, persistent, or weak value handles would leave caller-owned handles pointing into freed Runtime storage; teardown must reject that API misuse
exit_milestone: retain until value-handle ownership changes so Runtime teardown can safely invalidate or transfer every outstanding handle instead of requiring callers to deinitialize them first

contains: @panic("JSRuntime destroyed while execution or root frames are active")
reason: execution and root frames borrow Runtime state, so destroying the Runtime before they unwind would make deferred cleanup access freed storage; this is a teardown lifetime invariant
exit_milestone: retain until execution and root-frame lifetimes become owned or cancellable by Runtime teardown instead of requiring every active frame to unwind before destroy
